h2h_summary: England and DR Congo have no recorded head-to-head history, with 0 all-time matches played between them.
wc_history_home: England won the 1966 World Cup on home soil — their only major international title — and have since reached the semi-finals in 1990 and 2018, arriving at 2026 ranked 4th in the world seeking a second world title.
wc_history_away: DR Congo (then Zaire) made their sole World Cup appearance in 1974, becoming the first Sub-Saharan African side at the tournament but losing all three group games and conceding 14 goals; 2026 marks their dramatic return after 52 years.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: World-class attacking trio in Kane, Bellingham, and Saka capable of dismantling organised defences
High-press system that suffocates teams with limited technical quality in build-up
Dominant possession game with structural flexibility under Tuchel that can adapt to any opponent
strengths_away: Explosive counter-attacking pace through Wissa and Mbuku on the wings
Defensive leadership and experience through captain Mbemba (107 caps)
Strong physical presence and aggressive pressing in midfield
team_style_home: Under Thomas Tuchel, England play a possession-dominant 4-2-3-1 that morphs into a 3-2-5 in build-up, with high-intensity pressing, inverted wingers, and over 70% average possession recorded throughout qualifying.
team_style_away: DR Congo deploy a compact 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1, emphasising defensive organisation and explosive counter-attacks through wide forwards with exceptional pace.
recent_news: England right-backs Reece James and Jarell Quansah will miss the World Cup last-32 game against DR Congo with injury. England look to continue their dominant form when they battle Congo DR in a 2026 World Cup Round of 32 match.
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
